text: Walking between these two spaces, I sense a quiet internal dialogue rising. In the first room, there's a communal focus—people huddled, absorbed by a luminous screen, ideas oscillating in discussion. I feel both included and distant, as if ideas float just out of grasp, urging me to listen. In the second, I'm surrounded by words and vibrant design—a showcase for creativity and thoughtful research. Here, I feel my mind split. Part of me wants to analyze, draw connections; the other part stirs with uncertainty, reflecting on the fluid boundaries between understanding and curiosity. I am here, a participant and an observer, adrift in the ever-shifting conversation of ideas.
